body.title: Memory metadata.comment field — convention + writer-side helper
body.result: Memory.add_comment helper, .comment property, palace rule theigors/rules/memory-comment-convention. 11 new tests, 4144 regression pass. Audit/lint check deferred.
body.description:
Memory.metadata is a free-form dict; using metadata['comment'] for human-readable annotations (why this memory exists, gotchas, sources) is a zero-cost convention. Establish it formally:
- Add memory.add_comment(text) helper on Memory model
- Document in palace rule theigors/rules/memory or theigors/rules/memory-conventions
- Add audit/lint check that flags HIGH-inertia memory writes (e.g. inertia>=0.9) without a metadata.comment explaining why

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/models.py (helper), palace rule, optional new audit check.
**Design rules:** memory palace rule (theigors/rules/memory).
**Scope boundary:** in scope: helper + convention + flag. Out of scope: backfilling comments on existing memories.
**Test plan:** unit test for add_comment helper; integration test that the lint check fires on a high-inertia memory without comment.
